data.title: Bitcoin Open Interest Drops 31% as Analysts Call a Market Bottom and Eye $105k Breakout
data.description:
Bitcoin's open interest decline below its 180-day moving average signals a critical deleveraging phase that historically marks market bottoms, with futures positioning shifting bullish and analysts projecting a potential rally toward $105,000.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-open-interest-drops-31-as-analysts-call-a-market-bottom-and-eye-105k-breakout/

data.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Experience Declines as Nvidia Shares Fall
data.description: According to PANews, the S&P 500 index saw its losses increase to 1%, while the Nasdaq dropped by 1.56%. Nvidia's shares also fell by 2.3%.

data.title: USDC Treasury Burns Over 135 Million USDC on Ethereum
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert reported that at 23:41 Beijing time (UTC+8) on January 14, the USDC Treasury destroyed approximately 135.6 million USDC on the Ethereum blockchain.

data.title: Coinbase will launch Midnight (NIGHT) perpetual contract trading.
data.description: Coinbase announced that it will launch NIGHT-PERP perpetual contracts on or after January 15, 2026 at 9:30 AM UTC, subject to liquidity conditions being met. The contracts will be available to retail users in select regions and to institutional traders through the Coinbase International Exchange.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court Delays Decision on Trump Tariff Policy Legality
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. Supreme Court did not issue a ruling on Wednesday regarding the legality of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff policy, indicating that the fate of this significant economic policy will remain uncertain until at least next week. The court has yet to announce the timing of its next opinion release, which may occur next Tuesday or Wednesday when the justices reconvene. During the November 5 hearing, debates revealed skepticism about Trump's authority to impose tariffs under a 1977 law. A ruling against Trump could result in refunds exceeding $130 billion.

data.title: Federal Reserve Official Suggests Deregulation Could Lower Inflation
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that easing regulations could exert downward pressure on prices, providing another reason for the U.S. central bank to consider interest rate cuts. By 2030, it may be possible to eliminate 30% of regulations, potentially reducing inflation by half a percentage point annually. Deregulation is seen as a positive supply and productivity shock, offering the economy more capacity and alleviating price pressures.

data.title: U.S. Regulatory Easing to Support Federal Reserve's Policy, Says Fed Governor
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that the ambitious regulatory easing in the United States will aid the Federal Reserve in further implementing its accommodative policies.

data.title: A "traditional asset trader on the blockchain" has made a profit of over $330,000 by investing in PAXG, silver, and synthetic assets in US stocks.
data.description: According to Coinbob monitoring, a certain "on-chain traditional asset trader" focuses on investing in US-listed synthetic assets and precious metals such as PAXG (gold), xyz:SILVER (silver), and TSLA. The account has a total value of $1.3896 million and unrealized profits of $330,000. Its core holdings are: a long position in PAXG worth $3 million, currently showing a floating profit of $127,000 (+42.49%); a long position in xyz:SILVER worth approximately $1.82 million, currently showing a floating profit of $230,000 (+41%); and also long positions in xyz:GOLD and xyz:TSLA.

data.title: Sui Mainnet Experiences Network Disruption
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 14, an official announcement revealed that the Sui mainnet is currently facing network issues. The core team at Sui is actively working to find a solution. As a result, decentralized applications (dApps) such as Slush or SuiScan may be inaccessible, and transaction speeds could be slow or temporarily halted.

data.title: Sui: The mainnet is currently experiencing a network outage and is at a standstill.
data.description: Sui announced on the X platform that the mainnet is currently experiencing a network outage and is at a standstill. The Sui core team is actively seeking solutions. Decentralized applications (dApps) such as Slush or SuiScan may be unavailable, and transaction speeds may be slower or temporarily unavailable.

data.title: The U.S. Supreme Court did not rule on the legality of Trump's tariffs.
data.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Wednesday did not rule on the legality of President Trump's tariff policies, meaning the fate of this landmark economic policy will not be known until at least next week. The court has not yet announced a date for its next opinion, but it is likely to be scheduled for next Tuesday or Wednesday, when the judges will reconvene. Previously, in a hearing on November 5, arguments showed the court was skeptical about Trump's authority to impose tariffs under a 1977 law. A ruling unfavorable to Trump could result in refunds exceeding $130 billion. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Milan: Regulatory easing will put downward pressure on prices, providing a reason for interest rate cuts.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that deregulation should put downward pressure on prices, providing another reason for the US central bank to cut interest rates. By 2030, perhaps 30% of regulations could be eliminated, potentially reducing inflation by half a percentage point annually. Deregulation is equivalent to a positive supply and productivity shock, providing more capacity to the economy and easing price pressures. (Jinshi)

data.title: Stablecoin service provider Meld raises $7 million, led by Lightspeed Faction.
data.description: Stablecoin service provider Meld has raised $7 million in funding, led by Lightspeed Faction, with participation from F-Prime, Yolo Investments, and Scytale Digital. This brings its total funding to $15 million. The company currently provides stablecoin payment services to businesses and individuals, aiming to create a "cryptocurrency Visa" that supports the purchase and settlement of stablecoins, Bitcoin, Ethereum, or any other type of digital asset globally. (Fortune)

data.title: Polymarket Trader Gains Significant Profit Through Strategic Betting
data.description: According to Foresight News, a trader identified as hai15617 has achieved substantial profits on Polymarket, earning $92,300 within a few hours. The trader joined the platform today and executed ten predictions. One of these trades resulted in a profit of $99,779, marking an 887% increase, which covered all losses and secured gains. Instead of predicting Bitcoin's direction, the trader capitalized on significant short-term market mispricing, placing large bets on opportunities where odds were excessively skewed. On Polymarket, the impact of pricing errors outweighs the win rate.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Losses from Fraud Predicted to Reach $17 Billion by 2025
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, cryptocurrency analysis firm Chainalysis has released a report indicating that losses due to scams and fraud in the cryptocurrency sector could reach $17 billion by 2025. The report highlights a significant increase in impersonation scams, which have grown by 1400% compared to the previous year. Additionally, scams assisted by artificial intelligence are reported to be 4.5 times more profitable than traditional scams.

data.title: In the past hour, $128 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, with $101 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $128 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, with $101 million in BTC liquidations and $16.3014 million in ETH liquidations.

data.title: A whale reportedly sold 255 BTC and opened long positions in BTC, ETH, and SOL with 20x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the whale that sold 255 BTC has completely closed its short positions and switched to long positions in BTC, ETH, SOL, and FARTCOIN, with a total position value of $351 million. This includes: - 2,151.75 BTC (20x long position) ($209.69 million); - 29,823.51 ETH (20x long position) ($100 million); - 265,236.44 SOL (20x long position) ($39.07 million); - 4.64 million FARTCOIN (10x long position) ($2 million).
